plannedSlices.title: Map/Scenario GDExtension Persistence Foundation
sourceDocs: 2026-05-03 owner direction: maps/scenarios need structure and persistence; prefer C++ Godot GDExtension map system
plannedSlices.purpose: Define and then implement a typed, durable map/scenario persistence architecture so authored and generated maps are first-class assets instead of loose JSON/dictionary payloads embedded into scenario content and session saves.
currentInspectionFindings: RandomMapGeneratorRules.generate(input_config) returns large nested Dictionary payloads with scenario_record, metadata, staging, validation/report, and provenance rather than a typed map object.

ScenarioSelectRules launches generated skirmishes by handing the generated payload to ScenarioFactory.create_generated_skirmish_session(...), preserving no-authored-writeback and memory/session-only boundaries.
Authored scenarios are bundled in content/scenarios.json with scenario metadata, terrain rows, objectives, script hooks, and placements in one JSON domain.
SaveService._save_raw_dictionary() serializes full payload dictionaries with JSON.stringify(payload, "\t") and writes raw JSON strings through FileAccess.
A Small 36x36 generated map profile wrote about 6.95 MB of JSON and measured roughly 202-219 ms in the save path, so full-payload JSON saves are already expensive before larger maps.
implementationTargets: future C++ Godot GDExtension map package module and GDScript adapter API
completionCriteria: Define typed MapDocument and ScenarioDocument boundaries with explicit schema/version, stable ids, metadata, terrain/layer data, object placements, route/validation metadata, and generated provenance.
Select a durable map package format for authored and generated maps with load, validate, save, migrate, corruption/tamper handling, and deterministic identity/hash support.
Plan save files around immutable map package references plus compact mutable session deltas rather than full map/scenario JSON rewrites where practical.
Plan content/scenarios.json migration toward a compact manifest/index with separate authored/generated map package files.
Define a staged bridge from current GDScript RMG dictionary output into the new package format before any full C++ generator rewrite.
Name rollback, compatibility, validation scene/report, and performance acceptance gates before implementation begins.
baselineChecks: focused generated-map save/load, scenario-load, and RMG validation smokes selected at kickoff
nonGoals: No immediate coding or coding-agent implementation during planning refinement.
No breaking existing saves or authored scenarios without an explicit migration slice.
No full RMG rewrite as the first step.
No renderer, fog, pathing, occupancy, gameplay semantics, or production content migration unless separately selected.
No generated PNG/asset ingestion.
plannedSlices.notes: Native inert package API, Windows config/helper, and first native RMG foundation API/identity/stub proof are implemented and validated. 2026-05-03 Windows helper correction complete: build_map_persistence_windows.bat now checks Godot --version for 4.6.2 before focused smokes and documents --allow-other-godot-version for exceptional local testing. Validation passed: python3 -m json.tool ops/progress.json; progress sync-plan --dry-run; cmake native rebuild; map package smoke; native RMG smoke; git diff --check; python3 tests/validate_repo.py. Broad package read/write/conversion/adoption, save migration, content migration, full native RMG parity, and runtime call-site adoption remain future slices.

plannedSlices.title: Overworld Full-Route Movement Model
completionCriteria: Path preview precomputes cost for the full selected route and partitions reachable versus out-of-movement tiles.
Mouse/primary route confirmation executes movement along the selected route to the farthest reachable tile in the current movement budget.
Routes do not pass through blockers or intermediate interactions; interaction resolution only occurs when the interaction destination is actually reached.
Fog remains HoMM-style permanent explored visibility and expands along traversed route steps.
F3/debug command buckets identify full-route execution and retain movement timing evidence.
nonGoals: Do not change map generation, templates, player counts, object density/counts, object placement, objectives, save schema, renderer art semantics, or object body/visit contracts.
Do not add primitive/debug rendering, hidden downscale, content cuts, fake animation, or broad save/render/content migrations.
Do not weaken blocker, body tile, visit tile, or interaction destination contracts.
completionEvidence: OverworldRules exposes route_movement_preview and try_move_along_route. Preview normalizes a full route, totals cost, partitions reachable and out-of-movement tiles, and reports the farthest reachable final tile.

try_move_along_route validates that a route starts at the active hero, is contiguous, stays in bounds, avoids blockers, and does not pass through intermediate interactions; it consumes one movement point per traversed tile, updates the active hero final position, reveals fog along route steps, and only resolves the destination interaction when that destination is reached.

OverworldShell primary route confirmation now executes the selected route to the farthest reachable tile instead of advancing only one adjacent step, while single-step try_move remains available for keyboard/direct movement callers.

OverworldShell and OverworldMapView route selection/path building no longer reject cached-but-undiscovered passable destinations, but still reject blocked destinations and paths through intermediate interactions.

OverworldMapView draws the reachable route segment in the normal route style and continues the planned out-of-movement segment in the blocked/red style; validation_view_metrics exposes the route preview partition.
F3 command timing now records full_route_execute with route_execution_lookup and movement_rules buckets for primary route confirmation.

Focused route regression evidence: full-route preview total_steps=9, reachable_steps=4, unreachable_steps=5; confirmation executed_steps=4 to final tile 4,1, consumed movement to 0, preserved the unreachable destination, and reported full_route_execute total_command_ms=2121.124, route_execution_lookup_ms=0.004, movement_rules_ms=48.202.
plannedSlices.notes: Animation remains prepared through route_steps/route_execution payloads only; no fake tile-by-tile animation was added in this slice.
